authority: Official DMS catalogue material and governed Quivrr catalogue records

models.reason: DMS positions the Carny as a high-performance all-rounder for average to better waves, combining easy paddling and everyday range with a sharper performance response.
The pocket-rocket outline, low rocker, distributed rail volume and quick pivoting tail are explicitly directed at creating speed and manoeuvrability in small surf.
The Seahawk is a versatile twin-fin platform for one-to-six-foot surf, with speed, flow and easy rotation defining a broad everyday alternative rather than a fish-first identity.
Its mini-Simmons influence, playful pointy-twin layout, speed and compact fun-wave brief place it in the groveller lane despite its more refined rails and added performance range.
DMS describes the Supercell as a true high-performance shortboard with stronger rocker, deeper concave and vertical surfing intent for average to better quality waves.
The fuller outline, low rocker and broad range from weaker onshore surf to larger waves make the Thunderbird an explicit all-round performance daily driver.
The X-Wing adds a more aggressive channel and flyer concept to Supercell DNA while remaining deliberately more accessible and controllable than an ultra-demanding HPSB.
Its modernised 1970s single-fin design emphasises stable take-offs, trim speed, glide and smooth everyday turns rather than high-performance, fish or mid-length identity.

review_notes: All eight governed DMS models have an explicit public family and detailed category.
Gherkin and Stilnox were moved out of the broad Daily Driver fallback because compact small-wave speed generation is their primary role.
Supercell was moved to Performance Shortboard because DMS explicitly presents it as a true high-performance design.
Seahawk remains Daily Driver as an alternative twin because its broad one-to-six-foot versatility is stronger than any fish-specific design identity.
Construction technology such as Carbon Wrap or Injection Tech may change flex and wave suitability but does not change the underlying board family.
